methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Western Chemicals, a stealth-stage company in Climate & Energy founded by Jared West, presents a valuation challenge given the nascent nature of the sector and the company's pre-revenue status. The comparable universe consists of 19 companies operating in Climate & Energy, including 1 public peer (LNZA) and 8 private companies. Notable private peers include TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape, spanning various subsectors within energy and materials innovation.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. A search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than the minimum three datapoints required for reliable triangulation. The filtered peer set proved too sparse to support median-based pricing without risk of systematic distortion. The broader all-sector median approach would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of business models, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines across Climate & Energy.

Without sufficient comparable data to support quantitative triangulation, Western Chemicals' valuation cannot be anchored to conventional VC multiples or peer benchmarks at this stage. Valuation work would require alternative approaches—such as venture capital method analysis with explicit assumptions about exit timing, probability, and target returns—or qualitative assessment based on technology stage, team credentials, and market opportunity specificity. Further diligence on capital requirements and milestone trajectory would be prerequisite to establishing a defensible valuation range.
